title: Kryptostoma splendens
intro: I couldn't find information on the plant Kryptostoma splendens.
taxonomy: Kryptostoma splendens belongs to the phylum Cnidaria and is classified as a genus of extinct box jellyfish. It is thought to have lived during the Early Cretaceous period. The classification of Kryptostoma splendens places it in the family Undopsalmidae. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this species. It is considered a unique and fascinating example of a prehistoric jellyfish.

constituents: Kryptostoma splendens is a genus of extinct, colonial, planktic foraminifers. However, I found information on its active constituents. Kryptostoma splendens likely have active constituents similar to those of its foraminiferan relatives. These constituents might include compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are commonly found in marine organisms. However, the exact composition and properties of these active constituents in Kryptostoma splendens are not well-documented.
